result: If I could change the world in one way, I would erase the human capacity for indifference—the ability to witness suffering, injustice, or ecological collapse and feel nothing, or to rationalize inaction. Not to force constant anguish, but to ensure that awareness always carries a spark of responsibility. That shift alone would unravel systems built on exploitation, because no one could look at a child in poverty or a dying forest and simply turn away. Empathy without the option of silence would rewrite how we build economies, govern, and treat each other.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
